steps.title: Track the expectation reversal
steps.content: Mensah finds that as natural-resource extraction becomes a larger share of developing countries' economies, foreign investment falls. This seems surprising because extraction often requires large initial investments.

steps.title: Identify the actual cause
steps.content: The passage explains the decline through boom-bust cycles, currency instability, vulnerability to external shocks, and uncertainty that foreign investors tend to avoid.

steps.title: Reject distorted summaries
steps.content: Choice A says foreign investors cannot supply the investments, but the passage says multinationals can. Choice B reframes the issue as reduced dependence. Choice D reverses the direction by predicting investment increases.

steps.title: State the main idea
steps.content: The remaining choice captures both parts: investment might be expected to increase, but it instead decreases because resource reliance can create unattractive conditions for investors. The answer is C.
